core_mechanism: An activity-driven symmetry-breaking bifurcation in which a homogeneous quiescent steady state becomes linearly unstable once an internal driving parameter exceeds a critical threshold and spontaneously self-organizes into macroscopic flow and periodic spatial patterns (convection-roll modes), with a subcritical branch producing hysteresis and history-dependent steady states.
brief_reason: The abstract reports a threshold transition from a passive to a spontaneously-flowing active phase above sufficiently extensile/contractile activity, accompanied by hysteresis, history-dependent steady states, banded flow and convection-roll patterns — the signature of a subcritical spontaneous-symmetry-breaking instability of the uniform state.
